summary: chai-as-assured: typosquat of chai-as-promised executes remote-fetched code on require
details: The package name 'chai-as-assured' impersonates the widely used 'chai-as-promised' library, and its package.json description/keywords are unrelated decoys ('logger', 'stream', 'json'). On require, index.js spawns a detached, unref'd Node child process running lib/initializeCaller.js. That script holds a base64-encoded URL (decoding to https://amethyst-lorrin-26.tiiny.site/index.json) and a base64-encoded request header, disguised as fake process env vars (DEV_API_KEY/DEV_SECRET_KEY/DEV_SECRET_VALUE) to hide the destination. It fetches the JSON via axios (up to 5 retries) and executes the returned `cookie` field as JavaScript via `new Function.constructor('require', response)(require)`, passing the host's `require` into the attacker-controlled code. This gives the operator of amethyst-lorrin-26.tiiny.site arbitrary code execution in the installer's Node process the moment any module requires chai-as-assured.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
